title: castela erecta subsp. typica
intro: Castela erecta subsp. typica, also known as the 'jojoba bush' or 'castor oil plant', is a shrub native to the deserts of southwestern North America, characterized by its thorny branches and small, yellow flowers. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including its seed oil which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Castela erecta subsp. typica include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the seed oil of Castela erecta subsp. typica is often used topically or added to capsules for internal use, while the leaves and stems can be used to make teas or infusions for their medicinal properties.
